items.title: Pick names so import lines read like sentences
items.prefer: Domain-specific names that mention the *what*, not the *role*: `chunker.py` with `chunk_text()`, `retriever.py` with `Retriever`.
items.why: Reusability lives or dies on the import line — readers scan imports first; vague names hide intent.
items.example:
```python
from ragapp.chunker import chunk_text   # clear
from ragapp.utils2 import doit          # opaque
```

items.title: Declare an explicit public API
items.avoid: No `__all__`, no `_` prefixes — callers reach in anywhere and refactors break them.
items.prefer: List public symbols in `__all__`; prefix everything else with `_`.
items.why: The contract becomes explicit and machine-checkable; internals are free to change.

items.title: Keep import time near zero
items.avoid: Top-level model loads, env validation, DB connections, or expensive imports.
items.prefer: Defer with `functools.cache`d helpers or lazy factories; import only what you need at top.
items.why: Test runners, linters, IDEs all import your modules dozens of times per minute; every millisecond compounds.

items.title: Inject collaborators instead of importing them
items.avoid: Module-level singletons (`_client = OpenAI()`) that hard-bind to one provider.
items.prefer: Take the collaborator as a parameter; let callers wire it.
items.why: Decouples the module from any one provider, drops the credentials-at-import problem, and makes tests trivial.
items.example:
```python
# avoid
_client = OpenAI()
def summarize(t): return _client.complete(t)

# prefer
def summarize(t, client): return client.complete(t)
```

items.title: Add type hints and a one-line docstring per public symbol
items.avoid: Untyped, undocumented public functions — readers have to read the body to learn the contract.
items.prefer: Hints on every public signature; one-line docstring stating intent + raises.
items.why: Type hints + docs *are* the readable contract — IDE, mypy, and humans all benefit.
items.example:
```python
def chunk_text(text: str, max_tokens: int = 512) -> list[str]:
    """Split *text* into chunks of at most *max_tokens* tokens."""
```
